title: Report: Connecticut Sun to relocate to Houston with purchase by Rockets owners
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Connecticut Sun will relocate to Houston in 2027 after being purchased by Houston Rockets owners, the Fertitta family.
The WNBA franchise sale is reportedly a record $300 million, with the team likely renamed the Houston Comets.
Previous attempts by Steve Pagliuca to buy and move the team to Boston for $325 million were barred by the WNBA.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Daredevil: Born Again' Season 2 featuring Charlie Cox is now streaming on Disney+.
'From the World of John Wick: Ballerina' featuring Ana de Armas is streaming on HBO Max.
'Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice', a Vince Vaughn action-comedy, is available on Hulu.

title: Celtics’ Jaylen Brown (left Achilles tendinitis) out vs. Hawks
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jaylen Brown is out against the Atlanta Hawks due to left Achilles tendinitis, Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla says he's 'day-to-day'.
Brown recently scored 31 points in a win over the Thunder, contributing to Boston's second-place standing in the Eastern Conference.
Nikola Vucevic is out with a right ring finger fracture; Derrick White and Neemias Queta are available for the game.

title: ‘Where does it end?’: Readers react to gas-powered leaf blower bans
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Arlington and Lexington phase out gas-powered leaf blowers, prompting debate among residents.
73% of 250 Boston.com poll respondents oppose the bans, citing practicality and business impact.
Supporters cite noise and environmental concerns, advocating for quieter, battery-powered alternatives.

title: What to know about the highly mutated new COVID strain found in Mass. wastewater
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A new COVID variant, BA.3.2, nicknamed 'cicada,' has been detected in Massachusetts wastewater.
BA.3.2 was first identified in South Africa in November 2024 and has spread to 23 countries.
Current vaccines may be less effective against BA.3.2, but it hasn't caused increased hospitalizations or deaths, according to WHO.

title: DCF workers who authorized removal of kids in Waltham could stand trial, judge rules
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A federal judge ruled a lawsuit can proceed against two DCF workers for allegedly unlawfully seizing children in Waltham.
Judge Patti Saris allows claims against DCF's Katheryn Butterfield and Candice Gemski, who ordered the children's removal.
Parents Josh Sabey and Sarah Perkins argue their children's removal was based on coercive tactics without evidence of abuse.

title: 3 New England restaurants among the most anticipated openings, according to Food & Wine
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Willie's in Beacon Hill by chef Jamie Bissonnette serves pizzas and globally influenced pastas.
Ladyfish in Portland, Maine opens May 1, featuring Maine seafood with Chinese and Italian influences.
La Tavernetta in East Boston by chef Douglass Williams promises oysters and spritzes, opening this Spring.

title: The Boston Public Library is the star of Kate Quinn’s latest NYT bestseller
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Kate Quinn's new novel, 'The Astral Library,' stars the Boston Public Library as a fantastical portal.
Quinn, a Boston University alum, highlights Boston landmarks, drawing on her experiences living in the city.
'The Astral Library' debuted at No. 2 on the New York Times bestsellers list, shifting from her usual historical fiction.

title: Councilors, Pressley doubling down on demands for bodycam footage after deadly police shooting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston councilors passed a resolution urging police to release bodycam footage from a fatal Roxbury shooting for transparency.
Officer Nicholas O'Malley faces manslaughter charges in the March 11 shooting; he claims he acted in defense of his partner.
Rep. Ayanna Pressley supports the push for footage release, emphasizing the need for a truthful account and healing for families.

title: New England’s only dual-launch straddle coaster to open at Six Flags
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Quantum Accelerator, a dual-launch straddle coaster, opens at Six Flags New England on April 11.
The ride features two launches, high-speed turnarounds, and a stengel dive over a 2,604-foot track.
Quantum Accelerator is Six Flags New England's twelfth rollercoaster and the first new one since 2017.
